rationale: Ground truth is trivial (an 'lgtm' approval, no substantive comments), so recall ties. X is more precise and disciplined: it explicitly cross-checks the rename against the upstream vllm checkout, pinpoints the exact upstream line causing the original AttributeError, and correctly concludes APPROVE with no action items, matching the human 'lgtm' well. Y is nearly as grounded (accurate hunk-by-hunk validation, real grep evidence) but its three 'nit' comments are lower-value — one duplicates X's root-cause finding as a documentation suggestion, one flags the naming-convention divergence without recognizing it's the intentional point of the PR (upstream realignment), slightly undercutting its usefulness/precision. However Y's comments are far more concrete (specific file:line plus what to add/change), giving it a clear actionability edge, and it surfaces one genuinely novel, valid gap (no test for the chat-completion fallback path) that X never mentions.
